evidence: Docstring asks 'Are there k consecutive primes in arithmetic progression?' and the header references Wikipedia's Primes_in_arithmetic_progression#Consecutive_primes_in_arithmetic_progression.
verify_note: Decl Erdos141.erdos_141 exists with @[category research open]; QID live, label matches; header cites Primes_in_arithmetic_progression and statement is exactly consecutive primes in AP

evidence: The statement is via Set.IsAPAndPrimeProgressionOfLength, requiring the set of consecutive primes to be an arithmetic progression.
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the AP requirement (Set.IsAPOfLength) is the defining predicate of the statement

evidence: The progression must consist of consecutive primes (Set.IsPrimeProgressionOfLength built on Nat.Prime).
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the progression consists of consecutive primes (Nat.Prime is core to IsPrimeProgressionOfLength)

evidence: r_k(N) is defined as the largest subset of {1,...,N} with no non-trivial k-term arithmetic progression, i.e. the maximal progression-free (Salem–Spencer-type) set size.
verify_note: erdos_142 is the general k-term AP-free problem; Salem-Spencer set denotes the 3-AP-free concept, never mentioned in the docstring - only the k=3 variant (separate row) matches

evidence: Asking for an asymptotic formula for r_k(N), the AP-free subset maximum, is the quantitative refinement of Szemerédi's theorem (r_k(N)=o(N)).
verify_note: Szemeredi's theorem appears nowhere in the file or informal statement; it is background context for r_k(N), not load-bearing in the statement

evidence: The extremal quantity r_k(N) counts subsets containing no non-trivial k-term arithmetic progression (Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard).
verify_note: Decl exists with @[category research open]; QID live; 'no non-trivial k-term arithmetic progression' is the defining condition of r_k(N)

evidence: The k=3 variant asks the asymptotics of r_3(N), the maximum size of a 3-AP-free (Salem–Spencer) subset of {1,...,N}.
verify_note: Decl erdos_142.variants.three exists; QID live; r_3(N) is exactly the maximum Salem-Spencer subset size of {1..N}

evidence: Part (i) asks whether liminf |A ∩ [1,x]|/x = 0, i.e. whether the lower asymptotic density of the well-separated set vanishes.
verify_note: Decl erdos_143.parts.i exists with @[category research open]; QID live (alias 'asymptotic density'); the statement is literally whether the lower asymptotic density liminf |A∩[1,x]|/x vanishes

evidence: The problem concerns moments of gaps s_{n+1}-s_n in the sequence of squarefree numbers (Nat.nth Squarefree, OEIS A005117).
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the statement is about gap moments of the squarefree number sequence (Nat.nth Squarefree)

evidence: The series sum (-1)^n n/p_n runs over p_n, the sequence of primes (k.nth Nat.Prime).
verify_note: Decl Erdos15.erdos_15 exists with @[category research open]; QID live; the series runs over k.nth Nat.Prime

evidence: The question is whether the alternating series sum_{n=1}^infty (-1)^n n/p_n converges (Summable of a (-1)^(k+1)-signed sequence).
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the series is (-1)^(k+1)*(k+1)/p_k - convergence of this alternating series IS the question

evidence: f(n) is a minimum over all Sidon sets A of size n (IsSidon A); the erdosproblems.com tag is 'sidon sets'.
verify_note: Decl Erdos152.erdos_152 exists with @[category research solved]; QID live; f(n) is an infimum over Sidon sets (IsSidon A)

evidence: The counted quantity is isolated elements of the sumset A+A (reference [ESS94] 'On Sum Sets of Sidon Sets').
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the counted set is isolated elements of the sumset A+A (Pointwise A.1+A.1)

evidence: f(n) ranges over all Sidon sets of size n (IsSidon (A : Set ℕ)); the erdosproblems.com tag is 'sidon sets'.
verify_note: Decl Erdos153.erdos_153 exists with @[category research open]; QID live; f(n) ranges over Sidon sets of size n

evidence: The mean squared gap is taken over consecutive elements s_1<...<s_t of the sumset A+A (reference [ESS94] 'On Sum Sets of Sidon Sets').
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the mean squared gap is over consecutive elements of A+A (Finset A.1+A)

evidence: The question is whether A+A is well-distributed over residue classes for Sidon sets A with |A| ~ N^{1/2} (IsSidon hypotheses).
verify_note: Decl Erdos154.erdos_154 exists with @[category research solved]; QID live; IsSidon hypothesis on every A k is central

evidence: The distributed object is the sumset A+A: 'Must A+A be well-distributed over all small moduli?'.
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; the well-distribution claim is about A k + A k, the sumset itself

evidence: Lindström's result [Li98] 'Well distribution of Sidon sets in residue classes' concerns the Sidon sets A themselves.
verify_note: Decl erdos_154.variants.lindstrom exists with @[category research solved]; QID live; Lindstrom's statement is about distribution of the Sidon sets A k themselves

evidence: F(N) is the size of the largest Sidon subset of {1,...,N} (Finset.maxSidonSubsetCard) and the question is F(N+k) ≤ F(N)+1.
verify_note: Decl Erdos155.erdos_155 exists with @[category research open]; QID live; F(N) = maxSidonSubsetCard of {1..N} - Sidon sets are the whole content
